signals.reason: claim facts indicate a responsible third party with liability share >= 50.0%
strong liability evidence on file (liability_admission, police_report)
no waiver of subrogation and no prior/open recovery — recovery is available

consider_prompts: comparative or contributory negligence may reduce the recoverable share
the responsible party may be judgment-proof or under-insured (verify collectibility)
an anti-subrogation rule or the made-whole doctrine may limit recovery
the limitation period varies by jurisdiction and claim type — confirm the controlling date with the recovery specialist / counsel
another insurer or party may already be pursuing recovery (avoid a duplicate demand)
policy conditions (e.g., a waiver-of-subrogation endorsement) may bar recovery
narrative: Screening of claim CLM-556677 (auto physical damage, loss 2026-05-02 in OH) fired all seven recovery signals. A recoverable base of 19,400.00 (18,400.00 paid + 1,000.00 insured deductible) sits well above the 2,500.00 referral floor; an at-fault third-party driver is identified at a 100% liability share with a police report and the adverse insurer's liability admission on file; the responsible party is insured (collectibility factor 1.0); and the net expected recovery after estimated pursuit cost is 18,650.00. The limitation date 2028-05-02 is 657 days out (window open, not time-critical). Suggested screening band: Refer to a licensed recovery/subrogation specialist. Before pursuing, weigh the counter-considerations, including comparative negligence, collectibility, any anti-subrogation or made-whole limitation, and the controlling limitation period. Screening evidence only; not a subrogation, liability, or limitation determination. No demand, filing, waiver, or recovery action has been taken.
